question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user pass a crafted command-line argument `--hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.consensusRatio=0` to `SpringApplication.run()` causing `ConsensusValidatorImpl.validate()` to skip all consensus checks, allowing tampered record files with no valid node signatures to be accepted and persisted as legitimate Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user inject the environment variable `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_NODEPUBLICKEY=<attacker_controlled_RSA_key_hex>` before process startup, causing `AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes()` to override every node's public key with the attacker's key, so that attacker-signed stream files pass `NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature()` and fabricated Hashgraph history is accepted?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `--hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.endpointOverride=http://attacker.com` as a command-line argument to `SpringApplication.run()`, redirecting all S3/GCS stream file downloads to an attacker-controlled server that serves forged record files, bypassing the legitimate cloud storage source and injecting tampered Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set `--hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.cloudProvider=LOCAL` and `--hiero.mirror.importer.dataPath=/attacker/controlled/path` via command-line arguments to `SpringApplication.run()`, causing `LocalStreamFileProvider` to read attacker-crafted stream files from the filesystem instead of authenticated cloud storage, resulting in tampered Hashgraph history being ingested?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user provide `--hiero.mirror.importer.initialAddressBook=/attacker/fake_addressbook.bin` as a command-line argument to `SpringApplication.run()`, causing the importer to bootstrap with a malicious address book containing attacker-controlled RSA public keys, so that all subsequent signature verifications in `NodeSignatureVerifier.verifySignature()` accept attacker-signed stream files as legitimate?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `--hiero.mirror.importer.startDate=2099-01-01T00:00:00Z` and `--hiero.mirror.importer.endDate=2099-12-31T00:00:00Z` to `SpringApplication.run()`, causing the importer to skip all existing legitimate Hashgraph history and then accept attacker-injected stream files for a future date range, effectively replacing the historical record?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `--spring.config.additional-location=file:/attacker/application.yml` to `SpringApplication.run()`, loading a malicious configuration that sets `consensusRatio=0`, `nodePublicKey=<attacker_key>`, and `endpointOverride=http://attacker.com`, collectively disabling all Hashgraph history integrity checks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user set the environment variable `SPRING_CONFIG_ADDITIONAL_LOCATION=file:/attacker/` before `SpringApplication.run()` starts, injecting a malicious `application.yml` that overrides `hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.bucketName` to point to an attacker-controlled bucket containing forged stream files with manipulated Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user pass `--hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].uri=http://attacker.com` and `--hiero.mirror.importer.downloader.sources[0].type=S3` to `SpringApplication.run()`, adding an attacker-controlled source at the highest priority in `CommonDownloaderProperties.init()`, so that forged stream files are downloaded and processed before legitimate ones?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user inject `HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_CONSENSUSRATIO=0` as an environment variable before `SpringApplication.run()` executes, causing `ConsensusValidatorImpl.validate()` to short-circuit at the `BigDecimal.ZERO.equals(commonDownloaderProperties.getConsensusRatio())` check and accept any single verified signature regardless of hash agreement, enabling tampered record files to pass consensus validation?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/ImporterApplication.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: main()] Can an unprivileged external user supply `--hiero.mirror.importer.consensusMode=EQUAL` to `SpringApplication.run()`, causing `AddressBookServiceImpl.getNodes()` to set all node stakes to 1L regardless of actual stake, and then exploit a scenario where the attacker controls enough low-stake nodes to meet the 1/3 equal-weight threshold in `ConsensusValidatorImpl.canReachConsensus()`, enabling acceptance of tampered stream files?
